subject: Physics
solution:
Soap bubble has two surfaces. Work done: $W = \Delta A \times \sigma$
Initial radius: $r_i = 3.5$ cm = 0.035 m, Final radius: $r_f = 7$ cm = 0.07 m
Change in surface area (both surfaces): $\Delta A = 8\pi(r_f^2 - r_i^2) = 8\pi(0.07^2 - 0.035^2)$
$\Delta A = 8\pi(0.0049 - 0.001225) = 8\pi \times 0.003675 = 0.0294\pi$ m²
$W = 0.0294\pi \times 0.04 = 0.001176\pi$ J = $0.001176 \times \frac{22}{7} = 0.003696$ J = 3696 μJ
Therefore: $x = 15000 - 3696 = 11304$

subject: Physics

subject: Physics
solution:
Given: Radius of air bubble,
$$
r=0.1 \mathrm{~cm}=10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}
$$
Surface tension of liquid,
$$
S=0.06 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}=6 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}
$$
Density of liquid, $\rho=10^3 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$
Excess pressure inside the bubble,
$$
\rho_{\text {exe }}=1100 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}
$$
Depth of bubble below the liquid surface, $h=$ ?
As we know,
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\rho_{\text {Excess }}=h \rho g+\frac{2 s}{r} \\
&\Rightarrow \quad 1100=h \times 10^3 \times 9.8+\frac{2 \times 6 \times 10^{-2}}{10^{-3}} \\
&\Rightarrow \quad 1100=9800 h+120 \\
&\Rightarrow \quad 9800 h=1100-120 \\
&\Rightarrow \quad h=\frac{980}{9800}=0.1 \mathrm{~m}
\end{aligned}
$$

The nature of flow is determined by Reynolds
Number. 

Re=ρvDη

ρ→ density of fluid ;  η→ coefficient of velocityv→ velocity of flow D→ Diameterofpipe 

From NCERT 

If Re<1000 → flow is steady

1000<Rp<2000→ flow becomes unsteady

Re>2000→ flow is turbulent

subject: Physics
solution:
As liquid 1 floats above liquid 2 ,
$$
\rho_1 < \rho_2
$$
The ball is unable to sink into liquid 2 ,
$\rho_3 < \rho_2$ The ball is unable to rise over liquid 1 ,
$\begin{array}{ll} & \rho_1 < \rho_3 \\ \text { Thus, } & \rho_1 < \rho_3 < \rho_2\end{array}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Capillary height formula: $h = \frac{2\sigma\cos\theta}{\rho g r}$
When radius, density, and surface tension each decrease by 1%: $r' = 0.99r$, $\rho' = 0.99\rho$, $\sigma' = 0.99\sigma$
New height: $h' = \frac{2(0.99\sigma)\cos\theta}{(0.99\rho) g (0.99r)} = h \cdot \frac{0.99}{0.99 \times 0.99} = h \cdot \frac{1}{0.99} = h \cdot 1.0101...$
Percentage change: $(1.0101 - 1) \times 100 \approx +1.01\%$, which rounds to +1%.

subject: Physics
solution:
The amount of work done is equal to the change in the surface energy to increase the radius of the bubble.

The formula to calculate the change in surface energy is given by

 ΔU=2(T×ΔA)   ...1

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (1) to calculate the required work done.

∆U=3.5×10-2×4π(0.2)2-(0.1)2×2 J=3.5×10-2×4×227×0.03×2=2×1.32×10-2=264×10-4 J

subject: Physics
solution: $\begin{aligned} 1000 \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 & =\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 \\ 10 r & =R \\ R & =10 r\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} \frac{\text { S.E. of } 1000 \text { drops }}{\text { S.E. of Big drop }} & =\frac{1000\left(4 \pi \mathrm{r}^2\right) \mathrm{T}}{4 \pi \mathrm{R}^2 \mathrm{~T}} \\ & =\frac{1000 \times \mathrm{r}^2}{(10 \mathrm{r})^2}=10=\frac{10}{\mathrm{x}} \\ \therefore \quad x & =1\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
The buoyant force is given by 

B=Vρ0g

where V is the volume of the ball. The weight of the ball

W=Mg=Vρg, is acting in the opposite direction of the buoyant force . Hence, at terminal velocity

Mg-B=Fv

⇒B+Fv=Mg

⇒Fv=Vρg-Vρ0g⇒Fv=ρVg(1-ρ0ρ)⇒Fv=Mg(1-ρ0ρ)

subject: Physics
solution:
Volume of cube inside water
$\begin{aligned}
& =\left(\frac{\text { Denslty of cube }}{\text { Denslty of water }}\right) \times \text { Volume of cube } \\
& =\frac{\text { Mass of cube }}{\text { Denslty of water }} \\
& =\frac{400 \mathrm{gm}}{1 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cm}^3} \\
& =400 \mathrm{~cm}^3
\end{aligned}$
Volume of cube outside water
$\begin{aligned}
& =\text { Volume of cube }- \text { Volume of cube inside water } \\
& =1000 \mathrm{~cm}^3-400 \mathrm{~cm}^3 \\
& =600 \mathrm{~cm}^3
\end{aligned}$

VA=25×10-61.5×10-4×0.6

=110 m s-1

Substituting in equation (i)

⇒PA-PB=100020.62-0.12=10002×0.7×0.5

=175 Pa
